task: Color emoji glyphs render via the browser's own built-in colour-font tables and ignore CSS/canvas fillStyle entirely, so a literal per-region recolor of the library's 500+ existing glyph-based stickers isn't possible -- this ships the Auto Duotone technique instead, approved as an interim solution while a real vector-asset-based library stays an open question. renderer/slideRenderer.js's _buildRecoloredStickerCanvas reads the glyph's own light/dark pixels via getImageData and splits them into a Body tone + a Shade tone by luminance, plus a dilated Outline ring stamped from the same alpha shape -- preserving some of the glyph's own shape detail rather than flattening to one flat silhouette colour. _drawSceneSticker branches on a new recolorEnabled instance field; _stickerBbox gained a matching visual:{kind:'image',src} field so Object Strip shows the real recolored sticker. js/cardDesigner.js's sticker Refine panel gained a 'Colour This' toggle plus Inside/Shade/Outline colour inputs, seeding sensible defaults only on first enable so a later disable/re-enable remembers whatever was picked. Works automatically on all 500+ existing catalog entries, uniformly across Stickers/Decorations/Shapes since they're all the same instance shape, with zero new artwork. Verified via a new Playwright test (pixel-sampled canvas repaints, Object Strip thumbnail) plus full regression across the existing Creator/Builder suite and both goldenBuild.js suites, zero console errors. Confined to renderer/slideRenderer.js, js/cardDesigner.js, one CSS addition.
